channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Reseña de ‘Michael’: un filme biográfico que ignora los problemas de Michael Jackson
item.description: El filme presenta una serie de piezas musicales, como un álbum de grandes éxitos, que se repiten con notas falsas, resultando insultantes tanto para el público como para el personaje.
item.dc:creator: Alissa Wilkinson

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 25, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: How Jesse Tyler Ferguson of ‘Modern Family’ Is Showing His Range
item.description: Mr. Ferguson, best known for starring in the sitcom “Modern Family,” has leaned back into his theater roots with a turn as Truman Capote.
item.dc:creator: Addie Morfoot

media:content.media:credit: Ye Fan for The New York Times

item.title: Tim Cook hizo maravillas por Apple. También por China
item.description: El exdirector ejecutivo convirtió a Apple en un gigante, pero lo hizo arriesgando la seguridad económica y política de Estados Unidos.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGee

item.title: Charlize Theron: la violencia la moldeó pero no la define
item.description: La ganadora del Oscar reflexiona sobre el dolor, el proceso de sanación y su camino para convertirse en estrella de cine de acción.
item.dc:creator: Lulu Garcia-Navarro

media:content.media:credit: Devin Oktar Yalkin for The New York Times

item.title: Un perrito robot para cuidar a mamá en su vejez
item.description: Nuevas tecnologías de monitoreo ayudan a los adultos mayores a recordar que tomen sus pastillas o a entablar conversaciones con un robot mientras aligeran la carga para los cuidadores que no pueden estar siempre con ellos.
item.dc:creator: Susan Shain

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Carol DeMaio, de 80 años, que padece demencia, llamó a su perro robot Sabrina, en honor a una golden retriever que murió. Cuando DeMaio duerme, el perro robot permanece a los pies de su cama, reaccionando cuando se despierta.

item.title: España y el Reino Unido se oponen a posibles sanciones de Trump
item.description: Un correo electrónico interno del Pentágono, según informó la agencia Reuters, sugería que EE. UU. analizaba posibles sanciones contra ambos países por no dar el apoyo necesario para la guerra en Irán.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle and Emma Bubola
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: El primer ministro británico, Keir Starmer, el miércoles en Londres. Un correo electrónico interno del Pentágono sugería que Estados Unidos podría retirar su apoyo a la soberanía británica sobre las islas Malvinas.

item.title: Japan’s New Care Workers: Bodybuilders, Wrestlers and M.M.A. Fighters
item.description: Young athletes are being recruited to plug a staff shortage in Japan’s nursing homes. The results are reshaping elder care.
item.dc:creator: Chang W. Lee, Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times

item.title: The 85-Year-Old Widow Snagged by Trump’s Immigration Crackdown
item.description: In her first interview since being deported, Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, the French widow of a former G.I., recounted her experience in ICE detention.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter

media:content.media:credit: Dmitry Kostyukov for The New York Times

item.title: Japan’s Care Centers Recruit Some Muscle
item.description: Bodybuilders, sumo wrestlers and MMA fighters have started working at Japan’s short-staffed nursing homes in exchange for a steady income and gym memberships.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik and Chang W. Lee

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: April 25, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, April 25, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: With Chavez’s Legacy Stained, What to Do With Art in Tribute?
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, April 25, 2026.

item.title: U.S. Says Venezuelan Government Can Pay for Nicolás Maduro’s Defense
item.description: The issue had been hanging over the former Venezuelan leader’s federal criminal case for weeks. Last month, a judge indicated that he was skeptical of the U.S. government’s rationale for blocking the funds.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:description: Nicolás Maduro being escorted from a helicopter to be taken to the federal courthouse in Manhattan in January.

item.title: 2 Killed in Boat Strike in Eastern Pacific Ocean, Pentagon Says
item.description: The attack — the sixth this month — raised the death toll to at least 182 in a campaign against people the Trump administration accuses of smuggling drugs at sea.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gen. Francis L. Donovan, the head of the Southern Command, ordered the latest boat attack.

item.title: Helpful Pointer, Say?
item.description: Sam Ezersky’s scintillating Saturday puzzle sends solvers in all directions.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: A swan in Wales, wetting his beak before texting his exes.

item.title: SiriusXM Said to Be in Early Talks to Acquire iHeartMedia
item.description: A deal, which was described by two people familiar with the discussions, would unite two major providers of traditional and satellite radio.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch and Benjamin Mullin

media:content.media:credit: Amy Lombard for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The hosts of “SmartLess,” from left, Jason Bateman, Will Arnett and Sean Hayes, on a sofa after a taping in 2024. SiriusXM’s chief executive, Jennifer C. Witz, and chief content officer, Scott Greenstein, stood behind them.

item.title: Rubio’s Absence From Iran Talks Highlights Stay-at-Home Role
item.description: President Trump has outsourced much of his diplomacy to others, while Mr. Rubio focuses on his second job as national security adviser.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: As President Trump prepares to send a delegation to the latest round of U.S.-Iran talks in Pakistan, his secretary of state, Marco Rubio, will remain where he often does: at home.

item.title: One of Two Missing USF Doctoral Students Is Found Dead, Officials Say
item.description: The University of South Florida students were reported missing last week. A roommate of the student whose body was found was charged in connection with his disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A search continued on Friday for a missing University of South Florida doctoral students after the body of another student who had been reported missing was found on a bridge over Old Tampa Bay, officials said.

item.title: Kenyan Court Strikes Down Ruling Protecting Right to Abortion
item.description: The decision, in a country where thousands of women die yearly from unsafe abortions, held that abortions deprive unborn children of the “right to life.”
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar

media:content.media:description: Activists protesting in Nairobi in 2019.

item.title: Drivers Stranded Overnight on Alberta, Canada, Highways After Snowstorm
item.description: The Royal Canadian Mounted Police said they were working to rescue those stranded and deliver supplies like food, fuel and medicine.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

item.title: Wildfires in Georgia Destroy 120 structures
item.description: Two large wildfires in southeast Georgia had destroyed more than 120 structures and scorched tens of thousands of acres of drought-stricken land, according to state officials. The fires were among dozens that have been burning in the state.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: History of Domestic Abuse Can be Considered in Sentencing, Court Rules
item.description: The court ruled that prosecutors cannot make defendants who take a plea deal waive their right to a hearing that would allow their history of domestic abuse to be considered during sentencing.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:description: New York’s top court said prosecutors could not make defendants waive a right to a hearing that takes into account their history of domestic abuse.

item.title: Did an Acid Trip Inspire the First Photo of Earth?
item.description: How did looking back at the Earth for the first time change humanity’s perspective? The legendary thinker Stewart Brand tells the Opinion columnist Ezra Klein why we need to shift our focus from “looking outward” to the essential, constant work of maintaining our civilizations and the planet itself.
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: The Prophet of Silicon Valley
item.description: Stewart Brand, an author and visionary, deeply influenced the internet era from the 1960s onward. Yet the systems he inspired have become fundamentally unintelligible to their creators. The Opinion columnist Ezra Klein explores the tension between Brand’s “how to” philosophy and the black-box reality of modern A.I. on “The Ezra Klein Show.”
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’

item.title: Two Men Plead Guilty in Harvard Medical School Explosion
item.description: Logan David Patterson, 18, and Dominick Frank Cardoza, 21, pleaded guilty for the November incident in which they set off a commercial firework in a research lab.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao

media:content.media:credit: Spencer Jones/Universal Images Group, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The explosion occurred on the fourth floor of the Goldenson Building, part of Harvard Medical School.

item.title: Wildfires Destroy Dozens of Structures in Georgia, Governor Says
item.description: Fires fueled by drought have burned tens of thousands of acres in southern Georgia and northern Florida, where officials said one blaze was blamed for the death of a volunteer firefighter.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker and Amy Graff

media:content.media:description: The Highway 82 fire in rural Brantley County, Ga., has burned more than 7,500 acres and 87 homes and other structures, Gov. Brian Kemp said.

item.title: $100 Million Award Made in Suit Over Unlicensed Robert Indiana Art
item.description: A New York jury found that an art publisher who made works derived from Indiana’s images had infringed on the rights of a company that had been the artist’s partner.
item.dc:creator: Graham Bowley

media:content.media:description: Robert Indiana’s most famous work, “LOVE.” There are iterations of the sculpture in several cities, including this one at John F. Kennedy Plaza in Philadelphia.

item.title: 31 Sloths Acquired by an Orlando Animal Attraction Died, Officials Say
item.description: Many of the deaths were attributed to a lack of heat at an unauthorized warehouse that Sloth World was using, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

media:content.media:description: The sloths were going to be the latest attraction at Sloth World in Orlando, Fla.

item.title: The ‘Lasting Damage’ of Pirro’s Investigation of the Federal Reserve and Powell
item.description: The Trump administration’s attacks on the Federal Reserve have rattled confidence in the central bank’s ability to operate independently before a leadership transition.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith and Tony Romm

media:content.media:description: President Trump visited the Federal Reserve’s Washington headquarters in July during renovations.

item.title: Republicans’ Likely Strategy as Midterms Near: Negative Campaigning
item.description: Republicans are likely to fall back on a tried-and-tested strategy for the midterms: Going negative.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times

item.title: Wayne Moss, Guitarist Who Helped Broaden Country Sound, Dies at 88
item.description: A top Nashville musician, he played on Bob Dylan’s “I Want You,” Roy Orbison’s “Oh, Pretty Woman” and Dolly Parton’s “Jolene.”
item.dc:creator: Bill Friskics-Warren

item.title: Student Contest Based on Trump’s Garden of Heroes Downplays Darker History
item.description: Guidelines for the art contest, sponsored by the group Freedom 250, include material about abolitionists and civil rights leaders, but little mention of what they fought against.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Schuessler

item.title: Kushner and Witkoff Traveling to Pakistan to Resume Iran Talks
item.description: Iran’s foreign minister has already arrived in the country, state media reported. He was believed to be carrying a written response to a U.S. proposal to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Farnaz Fassihi and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tehran on Thursday. Iran’s foreign minister was expected to meet with Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner in Pakistan this weekend.

item.title: Over 200,000 Heated Socks Sold at Costco Recalled After Reports of Burns
item.description: The socks, from the brand 32 Degrees, were linked to reports of first- and second-degree burns, according to U.S. product safety regulators.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:description: The recalled socks were sold exclusively at Costco, where customers can return them and seek refunds, the company said in its recall notice.

item.title: Justice Dept. Closes Criminal Investigation of Fed Chair
item.description: Also, the U.S. is sending top envoys to Pakistan for Iran peace talks. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: President Trump with Jerome Powell at the Federal Reserve last year.

item.title: ‘No Warnings’: How Israel’s Deadly Airstrikes Unfolded in Central Beirut
item.description: On April 8, just hours into a ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran, the Israeli military carried out its most intense bombing campaign in Lebanon this year. The New York Times took a closer look at the deadly strikes in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Sanjana Varghese, Arijeta Lajka, Dayana Iwaza and Ainara Tiefenthäler

item.title: U.S. Unveils More Sanctions Targeting Iran’s Shadow Fleet
item.description: The Treasury Department also targeted a top independent Chinese refinery that it said was a major buyer of Iranian petroleum.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Kharg Island, Iran’s main hub for oil exports, in 2017. The Trump administration broadened its effort to squeeze Iran’s oil industry on Friday with a range of sanctions.

item.title: Sheldon Simeon Throws a Celebration for His Cookbook
item.description: The chef Sheldon Simeon celebrated his new cookbook with a low-key feast at an over-the-top venue.
item.dc:creator: Martha Cheng

item.title: Gambling Companies Celebrate Indictments as Pressure Grows
item.description: Gambling companies have cited recent federal charges as proof that illegal activity is being rooted out. The companies’ critics say the cases do not address widespread illicit activity.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich, Santul Nerkar, Meredith Honig and Benjamin Weiser

item.title: Does ‘The Drama’ Know Zendaya is Black?
item.description: Wesley Morris weighs in on Zendaya, her performance in ‘The Drama’, and on-screen interracial relationships.
item.dc:creator: Janelle Anderson, Felice Leon, Lisa Tobin, Jeremy Rocklin, Amy Marino, Alfredo Chiarappa and Daniele Sarti

item.title: Does ‘The Drama’ Know Zendaya is Black?
item.description: Wesley Morris weighs in on Zendaya, her performance in ‘The Drama’, and on-screen interracial relationships.
item.dc:creator: Janelle Anderson, Felice Leon, Lisa Tobin, Jeremy Rocklin, Amy Marino, Alfredo Chiarappa and Daniele Sarti

item.title: The World According to Aldwyth
item.description: In paint, bricolage and collage, she explored the history of art, ideas and the human species.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: Aldwyth, Ascetic Whose Artwork Reordered the World, Dies at 90
item.description: An austere lifestyle allowed her to make epic collages and Joseph Cornell-like contraptions that told stories about art history and her place in — and outside — it.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: 6 Shows Our Theater Critics Are Talking About
item.description: Rose Byrne in “Fallen Angels,” a couple trapped in the musical village of “Schmigadoon!” and “The Rocky Horror Show” at Studio 54: These productions are worth seeing.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Dulany

media:content.media:description: Rose Byrne and Kelli O’Hara in “Fallen Angels” at the Todd Haimes Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: Inside Trump and David Ellison’s Private Party in Washington
item.description: The gathering included executives and leading journalists from CBS News, which Paramount owns, and the head of the Justice Department, which is reviewing the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum

item.title: Judge Imposes Sanctions on Plaintiff and Her Lawyer in Suit Against Billionaire Leon Black
item.description: A federal judge said the lawyer representing an unnamed plaintiff who accused the billionaire investor of abusing her had “lied repeatedly to the court.”
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein

media:content.media:description: In a lawsuit, a woman said Leon Black sexually abused her when she was a teenager. He has denied all accusations.

item.title: 11 Standout Moments From Salone del Mobile
item.description: Milan’s annual design fair showcased contemporary creations, as well as midcentury homes not typically open to the public.
item.dc:creator: Monica Khemsurov

item.title: Why Are Politicians Dropping So Many F-Bombs?
item.description: Times reporters had a hunch that politicians were using the F-word more than ever. So they analyzed the data and found an interesting trend: Democrats are swearing far more than Republicans. Our reporter Kellen Browning breaks down why.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning, Mimi Dwyer, Yasu Tsuji, June Kim and Karl Mollohan

item.title: How Jet Fuel Shortages Could Affect Summer Travel to Europe and Beyond
item.description: Facing sky-high fuel costs linked to the war in Iran, airlines are cutting routes and raising prices. European vacations are looking a lot less affordable.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: The German airline Lufthansa cut nearly 20,000 flights over the next six months to conserve jet fuel, which could begin to run low in Europe as soon as next month.

item.title: Charlize Theron: ‘I Think Life Is So Beautiful’
item.description: On “The Interview,” the actor Charlize Theron says she may have a “tough” reputation, but at home her children tease her because she can cry at the drop of a hat.
